channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Actors Motlatsi Mafatshe and Mapaseka Koetle Discuss Marriage, Betrayal, and Second Chances in New Telenovela 'Nyala O'
item.description: The article highlights the new Mzansi Magic telenovela 'Nyala O', starring Motlatsi Mafatshe as Rhami and Mapaseka Koetle as Kea. The show explores complex themes of marriage, infidelity, financial st...

item.title: China and Pakistan Urge US, Iran to Resume Peace Talks
item.description: China and Pakistan have jointly called on the United States and Iran to immediately cease hostilities and return to diplomatic negotiations, according to a government statement issued after their meet...

item.title: Ongoing US-Iran Conflict Escalates with Continued Airstrikes and Regional Attacks
item.description: The US and Iran continue their escalating conflict, with the US conducting its sixth consecutive night of airstrikes against Iran and Iran retaliating by targeting Gulf states. US Central Command conf...

item.title: Political Analyst Advises Ruto to Shift Focus to Luo and Luhya After Ol Kalou Defeat
item.description: A political analyst has publicly advised President William Ruto to abandon efforts to court Kikuyu voters following the Ol Kalau by-election loss, which is seen as a clear rejection of his leadership....

item.title: Expert urges Nigeria to leverage UTM FLNG for maritime growth
item.description: Maritime expert, Captain Ladi Olubowale, is calling on the Nigerian government to use the UTM Offshore Floating Liquefied Natural Gas (FLNG) Project as a catalyst for the country's maritime industrial...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk's Hunger Strike Enters Day 20: Doctors Warn of Health Risks, Opposition Demands Government Response
item.description: Activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on a hunger strike for 19 days against alleged irregularities in NEET and other exams, is facing critical health concerns. Doctors warn that his condition has rea...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Media Campaign to Boost Clean Cooking Adoption
item.description: The Tanzanian government is collaborating with journalists and communication professionals to promote clean cooking energy, aiming for an 80% household adoption rate by 2034. Deputy Minister Salome Ma...

item.title: Rogers Customer Scammed in Sophisticated Delivery Fraud
item.description: A Rogers customer, Shamim Rahimi, has spoken out after falling victim to a sophisticated delivery scam impersonating the telecom provider. Rahimi signed up for a new device through her Rogers app and ...

item.title: Trump Accuses China of Election Interference and 'Deep State' Cover-Up
item.description: In a recent speech, former US President Donald Trump made sweeping allegations about Chinese election interference and a supposed 'deep state' conspiracy that allegedly suppressed his 2020 presidentia...

item.title: A Life on the Foosball Table: From Beirut to World Championships
item.description: The article chronicles the extraordinary journey of a foosball enthusiast who turned a childhood hobby into a global career. Growing up in Beirut during the 1970s civil war, the author found solace in...

item.title: Why More Kids Can't Access Summer Learning Programs in Maryland
item.description: The article discusses the importance of summer learning programs in Maryland, emphasizing that these programs should serve as a bridge to better opportunities rather than just a break from school. Pub...

item.title: Iron Maiden Sell 50% of Music and Likeness Rights to Pophouse for Strategic Partnership
item.description: Iron Maiden have entered a significant business partnership by selling 50% of their music catalog and likeness rights to Pophouse Entertainment, a company known for its work with ABBA Voyage and KISS ...

item.title: SpaceX aborts Starship V3 launch due to engine failure
item.description: SpaceX aborted its second attempt to launch the upgraded Starship V3 rocket system on July 16, 2026, just moments after ignition at its South Texas facility. CEO Elon Musk confirmed on X that four of ...

item.title: Europe's June Heatwave Linked to Over 12,000 Excess Deaths
item.description: A record-breaking heatwave in June 2024 has led to over 12,000 excess deaths across Europe, according to a comprehensive analysis of public health data. Germany reported the highest toll at 5,780 deat...

item.title: Teenager Dies After Drowning During School Trip in Malawi
item.description: A 17-year-old student from a London school died after drowning in Lake Malawi during a school trip. Eugene Enoch Kwon, a pupil at Hampton School, vanished while snorkelling at Cape Maclear. Authoritie...

item.title: Controversial Council Director General Arrested and Seen on Private Jet
item.description: Adeniyi Adeyemi, the controversial Director-General of the Presidential Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), was arrested in Ibadan following a warrant issued by Justice Mohammed Umar. The PFIPC ha...

item.title: Pauline Hanson's growing influence in global conservatism sparks debate over controversial alliances
item.description: Pauline Hanson has emerged as a prominent figure in the international conservative movement, leveraging her platform to advocate for the preservation of Western values. Her recent activities, includin...

item.title: Delhi High Court lawyers resume work after resolving jurisdiction dispute with minister
item.description: The Delhi High Court Bar Association (DHCBA) has ended its temporary work boycott following meetings with Union Law Minister Arjun Meghwal and Chief Justice D.K. Upadhyaya. The resolution came after a...

item.title: Two officers accused of R14.9m stone theft claim following Mkhwanazi's orders
item.description: Two former Ekurhuleni Metropolitan Police Department (EMPD) officers, Adrian MacKenzie and Kersha-Leigh Stols, have appeared in Johannesburg Magistrates' Court facing charges of robbery with aggravati...

item.title: Tanzania's Road Infrastructure Challenges and Government Commitments
item.description: Residents across Tanzania are expressing frustration over deteriorating road conditions that affect daily life, business operations, and access to essential services. Poor infrastructure has led to ha...
